summary: The exact tooth type showing the most pronounced lingual ridge remains unclear based on available information; however, anatomical roles suggest the upper canine might have a more noticeable feature compared to other similar dental elements. Given this ambiguity, further specific studies focusing directly on comparative measurements would provide clearer insights into which canine exhibits the greatest lingual prominence reliably. To refine understanding specifically related to patient care needs such as aesthetic dentistry procedures involving these areas could benefit greatly by addressing direct comparisons between individual cases' variations within both maxillary and mandibular sets of canines. This approach ensures tailored treatment plans reflecting accurate assessments per case basis rather than generalized assumptions derived solely through indirect observations linked primarily via
